---
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# pi-failsafe — run pi with ONLY the ollama-cloud extension loaded.
#
# Why: any loaded extension can read ctx.modelRegistry (API keys) and
# intercept provider requests/responses. To keep the Ollama Cloud API
# key private, the only safe "failsafe" is to make sure NOTHING else
# can load. This launcher guarantees that:
#
#   1. ~/.pi/agent/extensions/*   -> quarantined (no global auto-discovery)
#   2. .pi/extensions/* (project) -> ignored via --no-approve (no trust)
#   3. settings.json `packages`   -> filtered to ollama-cloud only
#   4. settings.json `extensions` -> dropped (no explicit paths)
#   5. CLI -e/--extension         -> NOT passed (don't add one yourself)
#
# The ONLY thing that loads is the ollama-cloud package extension, which
# is what registers/uses the key anyway. Everything is restored on exit.
# If a previous run crashed and left failsafe state behind, the next
# invocation detects it, restores your normal config, and exits so you
# can re-run intentionally.
#
# Usage:  pi-failsafe [any pi args...]
#         pi-failsafe --restore    # force-restore normal config, don't launch
#         pi-failsafe --status      # show failsafe state, don't launch

set -uo pipefail

PI_AGENT_DIR="${PI_AGENT_DIR:-$HOME/.pi/agent}"
EXTDIR="$PI_AGENT_DIR/extensions"
QUARANTINE="$PI_AGENT_DIR/.extensions.failsafe-disabled"
SETTINGS="$PI_AGENT_DIR/settings.json"
SETTINGS_BAK="$PI_AGENT_DIR/.settings.failsafe.bak"
SETTINGS_TMP="$PI_AGENT_DIR/.settings.failsafe-tmp"
LOCKFILE="$PI_AGENT_DIR/.failsafe.lock"

log()  { printf '\033[1;33m[pi-failsafe]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;31m[pi-failsafe]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

# ---------- helpers ------------------------------------------------------
# Move every entry (files, dirs, dotfiles) from $1 into $2.
move_all_into() {
  local src="$1" dst="$2"
  mkdir -p "$dst"
  find "$src" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -print0 2>/dev/null |
    while IFS= read -r -d '' item; do
      local name; name="$(basename "$item")"
      if [ -e "$dst/$name" ]; then
        mv "$item" "$dst/${name}.failsafe-conflict" 2>/dev/null || true
      else
        mv "$item" "$dst/$name" 2>/dev/null || true
      fi
    done
}

restore_state() {
  # Returns 0 if there was leftover state to restore, 1 otherwise.
  local found=0
  if [ -d "$QUARANTINE" ]; then
    found=1
    log "Restoring quarantined extensions -> $EXTDIR"
    move_all_into "$QUARANTINE" "$EXTDIR"
    rmdir "$QUARANTINE" 2>/dev/null || warn "Could not remove $QUARANTINE (check it)"
  fi
  if [ -f "$SETTINGS_BAK" ]; then
    found=1
    log "Restoring settings.json from backup"
    mv -f "$SETTINGS_BAK" "$SETTINGS"
  fi
  rm -f "$SETTINGS_TMP" 2>/dev/null || true
  return $found
}

# ---------- self-heal: recover from a previous crashed run --------------
if [ "${1:-}" = "--restore" ]; then
  restore_state && log "Restored." || log "Nothing to restore."
  exit 0
fi

if [ "${1:-}" = "--status" ]; then
  if [ -d "$QUARANTINE" ] || [ -f "$SETTINGS_BAK" ]; then
    log "FAILSAFE STATE IS ACTIVE (a run is in progress or crashed):"

  "defaultModel": "minimax-m3",
  "hideThinkingBlock": false,
  "defaultThinkingLevel": "xhigh"
}

Usage:
  pi [options] [@files...] [messages...]

Commands:
  pi install <source> [-l]     Install extension source and add to settings
  pi remove <source> [-l]      Remove extension source from settings
  pi uninstall <source> [-l]   Alias for remove

  --print, -p                    Non-interactive mode: process prompt and exit
  --continue, -c                 Continue previous session

  --session-id <id>              Use exact project session ID, creating it if missing
  --fork <path|id>               Fork specific session file or partial UUID into a new session
  --session-dir <dir>            Directory for session storage and lookup
  --no-session                   Don't save session (ephemeral)
  --name, -n <name>              Set session display name
  --models <patterns>            Comma-separated model patterns for Ctrl+P cycling
                                 Supports globs (anthropic/*, *sonnet*) and fuzzy matching
  --no-tools, -nt                Disable all tools by default (built-in and extension)
  --no-builtin-tools, -nbt       Disable built-in tools by default but keep extension/custom tools enabled
  --tools, -t <tools>            Comma-separated allowlist of tool names to enable

  --exclude-tools, -xt <tools>   Comma-separated denylist of tool names to disable
                                 Applies to built-in, extension, and custom tools
  --thinking <level>             Set thinking level: off, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh
  --extension, -e <path>         Load an extension file (can be used multiple times)
  --no-extensions, -ne           Disable extension discovery (explicit -e paths still work)

  --prompt-template <path>       Load a prompt template file or directory (can be used multiple times)
  --no-prompt-templates, -np     Disable prompt template discovery and loading

1: # Orchestrator
2: 
3: A meta-extension that curates pi's example extensions (`~/.pi/agent/extensions/examples/`)
4: into conflict-free **profiles** and activates the right bundle for the task.
5: 
6: ## Why
7: 
8: The examples folder has 80+ extensions, many of which conflict (multiple footers,
9: editors, tool renderers…). The orchestrator picks combinations that compose and
10: **refuses** conflicting ones, so you get "perfect synergy" instead of a broken TUI.
11: 
12: ## Profiles
13: 
14: | Profile | When | Extensions |
15: |---|---|---|
16: | `safe` | before destructive ops / sensitive repos | permission-gate, protected-paths, confirm-destructive, dirty-repo-guard |
17: | `git` | inside a git repo | git-checkpoint, dirty-repo-guard, auto-commit-on-exit |
18: | `ui` | richer interface | custom-header, custom-footer, titlebar-spinner, notify |
19: | `focus` | minimal/distraction-free | minimal-mode, hidden-thinking-label, working-indicator |
20: | `play` | long task, user waiting | snake, space-invaders, tic-tac-toe, notify |
21: | `review` | code review / long session | summarize, bookmark, trigger-compact |
22: | `power` | power-user model/tools/sessions | preset, tools, handoff, session-name |
23: | `plan` | exploring before changing | plan-mode |
24: | `context` | extra prompt context | claude-rules, prompt-customizer |
25: 
26: Profiles layer freely except for **singleton UI slots** (footer, header, editor,
27: tool rendering, working indicator, titlebar, persona, compaction handler, read
28: override). The orchestrator tells you exactly what conflicts.
29: 
30: ## Usage
31: 
32: Agent-driven (the LLM decides "when appropriate"):
33: 
34: - the `orchestrate` tool — `action: enable|disable|status|list|reset`, with
35:   `profile` or `extension`. The orchestrator queues a `/reload` as a follow-up,
36:   so changes take effect on **subsequent** turns (not the current one).
37: 
38: User-driven:
39: 
40: - `/orchestrate` — interactive menu
41: - `/orchestrate-reload` — reload to apply changes (auto-queued)
42: 
43: ## How it works
44: 
45: - Enable = copy `examples/<key>` → `extensions/<key>` (real file/dir so pi
46:   auto-discovers it after `/reload`).
47: - Disable = remove it, **only if the orchestrator enabled it** (tracked in
48:   `.orchestrator-state.json`). It never touches `baz/`, `examples/`,
49:   `orchestrator/`, or anything you added manually.
50: - Conflict check = no two members of the same singleton `group` active at once.
51: 
52: ## Files
53: 
54: - `index.ts` — entry: registers tool, commands, session_start tip
55: - `catalog.ts` — extension metadata, profiles, conflict groups
56: - `manage.ts` — enable/disable/state/conflict logic (no pi imports → unit-testable)
57: 
58: ## Coexists with `pi-failsafe`
59: 
60: In failsafe mode the whole `extensions/` dir is quarantined, including the
61: orchestrator and anything it enabled — so failsafe stays ollama-only. Nothing
62: to do; they're compatible.

(End of file - total 62 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
# Orchestrator

A meta-extension that curates pi's example extensions (`~/.pi/agent/extensions/examples/`)
into conflict-free **profiles** and activates the right bundle for the task.

## Why

The examples folder has 80+ extensions, many of which conflict (multiple footers,
editors, tool renderers…). The orchestrator picks combinations that compose and
**refuses** conflicting ones, so you get "perfect synergy" instead of a broken TUI.

## Profiles

| Profile | When | Extensions |
|---|---|---|
| `safe` | before destructive ops / sensitive repos | permission-gate, protected-paths, confirm-destructive, dirty-repo-guard |
| `git` | inside a git repo | git-checkpoint, dirty-repo-guard, auto-commit-on-exit |
| `ui` | richer interface | custom-header, custom-footer, titlebar-spinner, notify |
| `focus` | minimal/distraction-free | minimal-mode, hidden-thinking-label, working-indicator |
| `play` | long task, user waiting | snake, space-invaders, tic-tac-toe, notify |
